name: Stickynote Workflow
description: Automated workflow: Stickynote Workflow. This workflow integrates 8 different services: telegramTrigger, stickyNote, wait, telegram, code. It contains 32 nodes and follows best practices for error handling and security.

nodes.name: Initiate Static Data
nodes.notes: You only need to run the initialization step once per workflow, regardless of the number of Telegram chat IDs. The initialization creates the telegramStates object within the global static data of the workflow. Once that object exists, the workflow will use it to store the state for any chat ID.

nodes.name: Instructions Message

parameters.text:
=💡 Oops! That’s not a valid command.

Here’s what you can do:
✅ /start – Kick off a Pomodoro session and get in the zone.
✅ /stop – Wrap up your session like a productivity pro.

Now, let’s get some deep work done! 🔥💻

nodes.notes: This telegram node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.name: Sticky Note

parameters.content:
### 0. Initiate Workplace Static Data
Run it **once** before activating the workflow to initialize workspace data that will be used to **store state flags** and **outputs from users**.

#### How to setup?
- **Code Node:** do not change anything, just run it
  [Learn more about the code node]({{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }}

nodes.notes: This stickyNote node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.name: Sticky Note1

parameters.content:
### 1. Workflow Trigger with Telegram Message
1. The workflow is triggered by a user message. 
2. The second is checking if the message is a command (starting with "/") to route it to the proper block. If the message is not a command, the bot sends an instruction message to the user.
3. The third node checks if the message is a **/stop**. If yes, we stop the workflow the bot send a notice to the user and state variables are clears

#### How to setup?
- **Telegram Nodes:** set up your telegram bot credentials
[Learn more about the Telegram Trigger Node]({{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }}

nodes.notes: This stickyNote node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.name: start or stop?
nodes.notes: This if node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.name: Sticky Note2

parameters.content:
### 2. Deep Work Blocks of 25 minutes
1. The bot notifies the user that the session started.
2. After 25 minutes, it sends a notification to inform the user that [he/she] should take a break.
3. The loop continues until we reached four working sessions.

#### Why do we need Google Sheets?
Each deep work session is recorded to help users keep track of their stats.

#### How to setup?
- **Deep Work & Break Mode**: fix the amount of time you want for the deep work session (Default: 25 min) and the short break (Default: 5 min)
- **Telegram Nodes:** set up your telegram bot credentials
[Learn more about the Telegram Trigger Node]({{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }}
- **Record Deep Work in the Google Sheet Node**:
   1. Add your Google Sheet API credentials to access the Google Sheet file
   2. Select the file using the list, an URL or an ID
   3. Select the sheet in which you want to record your working sessions
   4. Map the fields
  [Learn more about the Google Sheet Node]({{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }}

nodes.notes: This stickyNote node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.notes: This telegram node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.name: Start Cycle Notification

parameters.text: ⏰ Time to focus! 25 minutes of deep work starts now.

nodes.notes: This telegram node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.name: End of Session Notification

parameters.text:
=🛑 You decided to stop the session early.
🚀 Use /start to relaunch a working session.

nodes.notes: This telegram node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.name: Sticky Note4

parameters.content:
### 3. End of the session for a long break
1. The bot notifies the user that the session ended.
2. The long break is recorded in the Google Sheets.
3. Variables are cleared so the workflow is ready for a new session with this user.

#### How to setup?
- **Telegram Nodes:** set up your telegram bot credentials
[Learn more about the Telegram Trigger Node]({{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }}
- **Record Long Break in the Google Sheet Node**:
   1. Add your Google Sheet API credentials to access the Google Sheet file
   2. Select the file using the list, an URL or an ID
   3. Select the sheet in which you want to record your working sessions
   4. Map the fields
  [Learn more about the Google Sheet Node]({{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }}

nodes.notes: This stickyNote node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

parameters.text: =🏢 Break over! Back to work for the cycle: {{ $json.count }}

=🛑 You decided to stop the session early.
🚀 Use /start to relaunch a working session.

=💡 Oops! That’s not a valid command.

Here’s what you can do:
✅ /start – Kick off a Pomodoro session and get in the zone.
✅ /stop – Wrap up your session like a productivity pro.

Now, let’s get some deep work done! 🔥💻
🍴 Time for a long break. Great job!
🚰 Work session complete! Take a short break.
⏰ Time to focus! 25 minutes of deep work starts now.

prerequisites: An active n8n instance installed locally, in a Docker container, or hosted on n8n Cloud.
Basic familiarity with importing JSON templates in n8n.
setupInstructions: Download the JSON configuration for this workflow.
Ensure you set up credentials for all connected integrations.
Test your trigger and run execution.
requiredCredentials: No custom credentials required for utility-only operations.
configurationSteps: Verify the settings of the trigger node "Telegram Trigger".
Open the "Sticky Note3" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "If Node" node (n8n-nodes-base.if) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Deep Work" node (n8n-nodes-base.wait) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Break" node (n8n-nodes-base.wait) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Record Deep Work" Google Sheets node.
Ensure you select the spreadsheet file and specific tab/sheet from the dropdown menus.
Map the input JSON keys to your target sheet columns.
Open the "Record Long Break" Google Sheets node.
Ensure you select the spreadsheet file and specific tab/sheet from the dropdown menus.
Map the input JSON keys to your target sheet columns.
Open the "Instructions Message" node (n8n-nodes-base.telegram) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note1" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "start or stop?" node (n8n-nodes-base.if) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note2" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Long Break Notification" node (n8n-nodes-base.telegram) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "< 4 Cycles" node (n8n-nodes-base.if) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Short Break Notification" node (n8n-nodes-base.telegram) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Back to Work Notification" node (n8n-nodes-base.telegram) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Start Cycle Notification" node (n8n-nodes-base.telegram) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "End of Session Notification" node (n8n-nodes-base.telegram) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note4" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Error Handler" node (n8n-nodes-base.stopAndError) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
No global variables node (Set node) is present. If you need dynamic constants, add a Set node after the trigger.
testingSteps: Click the "Test step" or "Listen for test event" button on your Trigger node.
Send a test trigger from your source service (e.g. fill a test form or trigger a webhook trigger).
Verify that the node canvas lights up green, indicating successful data flow.
Open the final destination node to confirm that output records were saved correctly.
instructions.expectedResult: Upon trigger activation, inputs will flow through the configured nodes and execute actions successfully.
troubleshooting: Check credential warning symbols. Most workflow issues stem from expired OAuth connections or missing API permissions.
Verify that the data keys coming from your Trigger node match the exact names mapped in subsequent nodes (e.g., {{ $json.body.email }}).
Open the Execution history tab in n8n to view logs and determine which node failed.
securityNotes: Never hardcode API keys, passwords, or authentication bearer tokens in the plain text fields of node parameters.
If you download or share this workflow JSON, sanitize any sensitive parameters or configuration nodes.
customizationIdeas: Add an email node at the end to notify you of successful executions.
Incorporate an AI LLM node to summarize trigger payloads before writing them to integrations.
